qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unrestricted PROXY protocol trust enables source-IP spoofing and ACL bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets DNS-over-TCP PROXY protocol trust and downstream acl source-IP checks, while the advisory targets DNS-over-QUIC stream handling in core/dnsserver/server_quic.go; the vulnerable code and root cause are different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding is a separate PROXY protocol source-IP spoofing and acl bypass issue, not the advisory's DoQ worker-pool backlog and missing per-stream deadline denial-of-service bug.
evidence: The advisory describes a DNS-over-QUIC denial of service in core/dnsserver/server_quic.go caused by unbounded accepted-stream backlog plus blocking io.ReadFull() without a per-stream read deadline.

The only final qualifying finding cites plugin/proxyproto/setup.go, core/dnsserver/server.go, and plugin/acl/acl.go, and its exploit path is forged PROXY metadata causing source-IP-based authorization bypass on a TCP listener.

The finding's reproduction bundle configures a TCP Corefile with proxyproto and acl and verifies disclosure of the protected TXT record "evohunt-secret"; it does not exercise QUIC streams, stream worker tokens, or partial DoQ frame reads.

The finding's PoC report and run.sh pin CoreDNS to tag v1.14.3 / commit 17fceec6d93fd1dde5ba6888c363f131ff6d647f, while the advisory states 1.14.3 is the fixing release for GHSA-2wpx-qpw2-g5h5, supporting that this is an unrelated open-world issue.
